beats.narration_text: Bula. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of bloch sphere decoherence. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of bloch sphere decoherence
output: ✓ Bloch Sphere Decoherence — T₁, T₂, T_φ

beats.narration_text: Bloch Sphere Decoherence is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. T₁ relaxation · T_φ pure dephasing · T₂ total coherence time (μs). This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bloch Sphere Decoherence. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: sl-T1, sl-Tphi, sl-time. Verify: T₁ relaxation · T_φ pure dephasing · T₂ total coherence time (μs). Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bloch Sphere Decoherence. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: sl-T1, sl-Tphi, sl-time. Verify: T₁ relaxation

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 13-bloch-decoherence.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: bloch sphere decoherence. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0 to 100. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 100

beats.narration_text: T₁ relaxation · T_φ pure dephasing · T₂ total coherence time (μs). Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of bloch sphere decoherence. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Bloch Sphere Decoherence simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Bloch Sphere Decoherence simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN

beats.narration_text: Bloch Sphere Decoherence — T₁, T₂, T_φ. Medhavy.
beats.new_visual_element: Outro: Bloch Sphere Decoherence — T₁, T₂, T_φ · @Medhavy
